ground_truth.title: Guide to Boards 2026: No Computer Required
ground_truth.author: David Groom
ground_truth.main_content: Microcontrollers and single board computers continue to get better as 2026 barrels toward us with more power and more capabilities that are perfect for amping up your next build. And while the big names like Arduino and Raspberry Pi have just released some exciting new products, they're not the only game in town. Here are 15 standout boards that have us excited for 2026. The Raspberry Pi 500+ is a Single Keyboard Computer - the first consumer-ready, plug-and-play model with on-board storage and a pre-installed OS image, featuring a built-in mechanical keyboard with hackable RP2040 powering LED animations. The Arduino UNO Q is a Dual-Brain SBC+MCU Hybrid that adds a powerful system-on-chip to the usual microcontroller's form factor, allowing use of familiar shields and libraries while paired with a full-blown Linux machine for running AI models or hosting a web server. The Unihiker K10 is an AI-Capable Smart Display with integrated Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, microphone, speaker, sensors, and a built-in 2.8-inch touchscreen, powered by the ESP32-S3 MCU. The Adafruit Fruit Jam is a Retro-Inspired SBC using the RP2350's Programmable I/O to create a business-card-sized computer running CircuitPython. The Bela Gem Multi is a Pocket Audio Workstation pairing PocketBeagle 2 power with 10 channels of high-resolution audio and a browser-based IDE. The D-Robotics RDK X5 offers Raspberry Pi form-factor with a 10 TOPs NPU at similar cost to Pi 5 without requiring an additional AI HAT. The OpenMV AE3 is a Compact AI Cam with dual-core Arm Cortex-M55 and dual Ethos-U55 NPUs providing quarter-sized performance comparable to multi-watt Jetson Nano boards. The IceWhale ZimaBoard 2 is a Pocket-sized Server with Intel N150 processor designed for media server, NAS, or homelab deployment. The Luckfox Lyra Pi B W is a Parsimonious Pi-like Platform offering a triple-core Arm Cortex-A7 with 512MB at lower cost than Raspberry Pi models. The Jumperless V5 is a Bonkers boujee breadboard featuring RP2350B with probe, clickwheel, 451 RGB LEDs, OLED display capability, and even runs Doom. The Pimoroni Presto is a Delightful Desktop Display and RP2350-powered modern successor to the Chumby with 4-inch touchscreen and RGB ambient lighting. The CrowPanel E-paper HMI Display features a 272×792 electrophoretic display with anti-glare coating, consuming power only when refreshing, ideal for low-power dashboards and home automation displays. The M5Stack Tab5 is an Itinerant Overachieving Terminal with 1280×720 display, front-facing camera, and 400MHz dual-core ESP32-P4, using NP-F550 camcorder battery standard. The LilyGo T-Deck Plus is an Off-grid Neo-Blackberry with LoRa, GPS, trackball, and backlit keyboard for Meshtastic communication without needing a phone. The Seeed Wio Tracker L1 Pro is a Minimalist Mobile Mesh device with LoRa, GPS, and OLED display for the perfect blend of consumer readiness, hackability, portability, and signal strength.
with: Microcontrollers and single board computers continue to get better as 2026 barrels toward us with more power and more capabilities that are perfect for amping up your next build.

For the first time ever, Raspberry Pi has released an actual computer! They've been doing similar since 2012, but this is the first consumer-ready, plug-and-play model with on-board storage and a pre-installed OS image.
When is an Uno not an Uno? When you add a powerful system-on-chip (SoC) to the usual microcontroller's (MCU) form factor.
Another glorious canvas for your imagination, the CrowPanel features a gorgeous 272×792 electrophoretic display (EPD) with anti-glare coating for incredible readability under natural lighting.
I've been experimenting with LoRa for years, but it wasn't until I encountered Meshtastic via Seeed's T1000-E that it became part of my daily life.
